output.dependency: A route/segment identifier in column 2 corresponds to a specific scheduled departure time/date in column 3 (the same itinerary identifier appears with consistent departure values).
The same route/segment identifier in column 2 corresponds to a specific scheduled arrival time/date in column 4 (arrival values align with the route identifier).

Column 4 (arrival time/date) is determined by column 3 (departure time/date) for the same trip segment, implying a consistent duration/ordering relationship (arrival occurs after departure for the same itinerary).

Columns 5 and 6 represent two time formats for related observed/planned times (e.g., actual vs scheduled or two recorded timestamps) that are paired within the same record and follow a consistent correspondence.
The route/segment identifier in column 2 determines the associated observed/planned departure timestamp recorded in column 5 (the timestamp format/value pair is consistent for the same route).
The route/segment identifier in column 2 determines the associated observed/planned arrival timestamp recorded in column 6 (the timestamp format/value pair is consistent for the same route).
